ogTitle: Cotswold vs Cottles Bridge: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cotswold and Cottles Bridge.
heading: Cotswold vs Cottles Bridge — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cotswold, Victoria and Cottles Bridge, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cottles Bridge has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Cotswold (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cotswold or Cottles Bridge safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cotswold has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Cottles Bridge scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cotswold scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Cottles Bridge scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cotswold or Cottles Bridge?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cottles Bridge has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
